⚠️ Software Sector Signal

71.5% of software names are negative YTD. This is not a sector rotation story — this is structural weakness. The AI-driven rally of 2026 has bypassed most software names. Only cybersecurity (FTNT), infrastructure monitoring (DDOG), and a handful of AI-adjacent software names are participating.

Stay away from the sector until UVCMI 777™ signals a turnaround at least on the 1H timeframe. The IT-LT screens show deeply negative weekly readings across the bottom half of the universe — these are not dips, they are downtrends.

§2 — Full Universe · UVCMI 777™ All Timeframes

All 63 Symbols · The Complete Multi-Timeframe Picture

The full software universe across all 7 UVCMI 777™ timeframes simultaneously. The sea of red is unmistakable — only a handful of names show consistent green across multiple frames.

Dr. UV's Software Tracker · UVCMI 777™ All TFs · Bottom 25 of 63 symbols · June 7, 2026

📊 Full Universe Read — The Structural Weakness

Top tier (FTNT, DDOG, CRWD, OKTA): The cybersecurity names show green or near-green across multiple timeframes. These are the AI-adjacent software names — security, observability, and identity management — that benefit from the same AI infrastructure buildout driving NVDA/SOXX. They are the exception, not the rule.

The massive red zone: From roughly symbol #25 downward, the UVCMI readings turn uniformly red across all timeframes. This is not a short-term blip — the weekly, daily, and intraday frames are all aligned negative. Multi-timeframe convergence to the downside = highest probability the downtrend continues.

Bottom tier (GTM, YEXT, INTU, KYVO): Weekly UVCMI readings in the thousands of negative bps — these names have been in sustained distribution for months. The AI revolution has made their legacy SaaS business models less competitive. Avoid until fundamental reversal confirmed by UVCMI weekly turning positive.

§3 — B Screen · Full Rankings

63 Symbols Ranked by YTD Performance

Dr. UV's Software Tracker · B Screen Part 1 · B1 Y/Q/M/W/D/2H/1H · Top 40 symbols · June 7, 2026
Dr. UV's Software Tracker · B Screen Part 2 · B1 Y/Q/M/W/D/2H/1H · Bottom 23 symbols · June 7, 2026

📊 B Screen Read — What the Rankings Tell You

FTNT leads at +8,109 YTD: Fortinet's cybersecurity platform benefits directly from AI-driven security demand. Every AI deployment requires security infrastructure. FTNT's multi-timeframe UVCMI structure (W: +2,776) confirms this is a sustained trend, not a one-quarter anomaly.

DDOG +7,023 · CRWD +4,186 · OKTA +3,659: The observability and identity security cluster. These names have AI-native features (Datadog's LLM monitoring, CrowdStrike's Charlotte AI) that the market is pricing as genuine AI beneficiaries. Their B1 Y readings confirm the market's verdict.

The QQQ/SPY comparison: QQQ B1 Y: +1,400 · SPY B1 Y: +779 — both benchmarks sit at roughly the midpoint of the software universe. Software as a sector is massively bifurcated: the top names crush the index, the bottom names crush your portfolio.

NET (Cloudflare) +2,478: AI-adjacent CDN and security infrastructure. Strong YTD but showing weakness in recent W/D readings — watch for potential deterioration if broader tech selloff continues.

GTM −7,061 · YEXT −5,643 · INTU −5,537: Legacy SaaS names being disrupted by AI. ZoomInfo (GTM) faces direct competition from AI-powered sales intelligence tools. Yext faces AI search displacement. Intuit is being questioned on Copilot tax/accounting competition. The market has delivered its verdict on these names — and UVCMI 777™ confirms it across all timeframes.

§4 — ST Screen · 1H / 2H / 4H

Short-Term UVCMI — Intraday Pressure Continues

Dr. UV's Software Tracker · ST Screen Part 1 · UVCMI 1H/2H/4H · Top 40 symbols · June 7, 2026
Dr. UV's Software Tracker · ST Screen Part 2 · UVCMI 1H/2H/4H · Bottom 23 symbols · June 7, 2026

📊 ST Screen Read

SOXX 1H: −516: The semiconductor Lead Scout remains deeply negative in short-term frames — confirming the broader tech selloff is not yet over. Software names track SOXX direction with a slight lag.

FTNT 1H: −27 (least negative of leaders): Even the top leader FTNT is showing slight intraday weakness. Not alarming at this magnitude, but confirms no sector is immune to the current risk-off environment.

YEXT 1H: −1,199 (worst ST reading): Catastrophic short-term momentum — sellers are actively piling in on intraday timeframes. When YTD B1 is −5,643 AND 1H is −1,199, there is no timeframe showing support. Complete avoid.

GTM 1H deeply negative: ZoomInfo showing extreme selling pressure across all ST frames. The weekly B1 of −10,888 is the worst in the entire software universe — and intraday confirms the distribution is ongoing, not pausing.

GTLB (GitLab) 1H: +47 — only positive ST reading in bottom half: Worth watching. GitLab's AI DevSecOps platform may be finding buyers. If 1H/2H remain positive while D/W rebuild, this could be an early recovery signal.

§5 — IT-LT Screen · D / 2D / 3D / W

Intermediate-Long Term — Weekly Structure Confirms the Trend

Dr. UV's Software Tracker · IT-LT Screen Part 1 · UVCMI D/2D/3D/W · Top 40 symbols · FTNT W: +2,776 · June 7, 2026
Dr. UV's Software Tracker · IT-LT Screen Part 2 · UVCMI D/2D/3D/W · Bottom 23 symbols · GTM W: −10,888 · June 7, 2026

📊 IT-LT Screen Read — The Weekly Verdict

FTNT W: +2,776: The strongest weekly UVCMI in the software universe. Fortinet's intermediate-term trend is firmly intact. Even if short-term weakness continues from the broader tech selloff, the weekly structure provides a strong cushion before any trend-change signal would fire.

DDOG W: +837.7 · SOXX W: +2,091: Both showing positive weekly readings — the AI-infrastructure cluster maintains intermediate-term bull structure despite the June 5 selloff.

GTM W: −10,888 — the worst weekly reading in the universe: ZoomInfo's weekly UVCMI is catastrophic. This is months of sustained distribution reflected in the weekly. No bounce is tradeable until W turns positive — and at −10,888, that requires substantial sustained buying.

INTU W: −5,250 · ZS W: −3,927 · YEXT W: −3,431: All showing deep negative weekly readings. These names are in intermediate-term downtrends that will not resolve quickly. The IT-LT screen is the verdict that matters most for position traders — and this screen says AVOID the bottom 30 names.

MDB (MongoDB) positive outlier: MDB showing positive W despite negative YTD — this is a potential bottoming pattern. Watch for D/W to both confirm positive before adding. Atlas cloud database has AI vector search capabilities that may be getting priced in.

🎯 Key Takeaway — Software Sector Strategy

Only own the top tier: FTNT, DDOG, CRWD, OKTA, RNG — these AI-adjacent cybersecurity and infrastructure names are the only software holdings with positive UVCMI structure across multiple timeframes. Everything below QQQ in the rankings (symbol #13 and below) has negative YTD and deteriorating IT-LT UVCMI.

Avoid the legacy SaaS cluster: GTM, YEXT, INTU, KYVO, BL, ASAN, HUBS — multi-timeframe UVCMI aligned negative. Wait for NYSE MO to recover AND weekly UVCMI to turn positive before any re-entry consideration.

Watch GTLB and MDB: These are the only names in the bottom half showing any early recovery signals (GTLB positive ST, MDB positive W). Monitor closely on the 1H timeframe for sustained green clusters as potential early re-entry signals.

UVCMI 777™ is Dr. UV's Master Indicator calculated from a single proprietary formula incorporating the last 60 units of closing prices. Runs across 7 simultaneous timeframes (1H, 2H, 4H, D, 2D, 3D, W). Green = positive momentum · Red = negative momentum.
